entries.title: Matt Olson plays in 741st straight game with Braves to become the franchise's Iron Man
entries.description: Atlanta Braves first baseman Matt Olson made franchise history on Friday night against the St. Louis Cardinals when he played in his 741st consecutive game for the team to break Dale Murphy’s record of 740 set from 1981 to 1986.  Olson signed with Atlanta as a free agent ahead of the 2022 season after playing parts of six seasons with the Oakland Athletics.  “It’s quite an accomplishment,” Braves manager Walt Weiss said.

entries.title: McGlynn flattered by Scotland links but focused on Falkirk
entries.description: Falkirk boss John McGlynn is flattered to be touted for the Scotland job but insists he is fully focused on the Bairns.  After back-to-back promotions that saw Falkirk rise from League One to the Scottish Premiership in two years, the 64-year-old led the side to a top-six finish in their first season back in the top flight.  Given the impressive job McGlynn has done, former Scotland boss Craig Levein suggested McGlynn would be an excellent pick to succeed Steve Clarke as the national team manager.
